one_time_description: Realized gain on portfolio investments of $1,000,000
economic_notes: First Nation operates multiple businesses including pharmacy, grocery store, fuel sales, entertainment centre, gaming commission, and housing authority. Video Lottery Terminal revenue of $1,951,400 net of payouts. 12.5% beneficial ownership in Shawano Wapunong Investments Inc.
notes: Qualified audit opinion due to non-identification of Asset Retirement Obligation (ARO) liabilities under PS 3280. Financial statements restated for prior year error corrections (Note 17) reducing opening accumulated surplus by $2,947,388 in 2023 and $1,689,328 in 2022. 12 entities fully consolidated including multiple businesses. Realized gain on portfolio investments of $1,000,000 is a significant other item. CMHC replacement reserve is underfunded by $166,466. Promissory notes of $2,190,033 relate to legal claims against Government of Canada. Economic Development segment expenses dropped dramatically from $5,360,354 to $917,510 year over year. Community Support segment (COVID-19) increased from $1,029,290 to $2,858,155.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
